name: Sphere POP
description: The Sphere POP creates spherical shapes in two different forms: Geodesic type creates a set of triangles of equally-spaced points which can be recursively divided giving more detail, and Grid type where you specify rows and columns of points similar to latitude/longitude lines. In both cases, the points can be connected with a variety of primitives using the Connectivity menu. Three radii parameters give ellipsoid shapes. The Detail page gives partial spheres spanning a start-end range of latitude and longitude. The Connectivity menu lets you output triangles, alternating triangles, quads, lines, line strips, point primitives or no primitives (None, only points). The sphere is symmetric around an axis, Y by default. In Grid mode where you specify the number of columns and rows, Columns run along the longitude lines from top pole to bottom pole, Rows run along the circles of latitude. In the Connectivity menu you can create lines and line strips along Rows, Columns or both merged together (Rows and Columns). The sphere can be post-rotated and post-translated using the Rotate and Translate parameters. Anchor will line up the left, center or right side of the sphere to X=0. Similar for Y and Z. The Details page lets you make a partial sphere by specifying a latitude (V) and longitude (U) range with any starting/ending angle. Dimension: When the sphere is passed to other POPs it cannot be known for certain how many rows and columns were specified. However the Sphere POP (and other generator POPs) output its [[Dimension]]s as metadata to all POPs connected to it, indicating its organizations of points in the points list. A sphere in Grid mode has dimensions ''numCols numRows'', which can be used by built-in attributes like _Dim[] or by GLSL code. You can output point or vertex normals (creating the N attribute), and you can output point or vertex texture coordinates (Tex attribute). The Texture Method menu lets you make Equirectangular or Fish Eye projections, correct for inside or outside the sphere. The Sphere POP takes an input which will cause the starting sphere to have the same bounding box as the bounding box of the input. Then turning on Modify Bounds lets you further transform the sphere. See also [[Grid POP]], [[Tube POP]], [[Torus POP]], [[Dimension]], [[Field POP]], [[Point Generator POP]], [[Revolve POP]]

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: Determines the sphere type.

parameters.name: Geodesic Connectivity
parameters.label: Geodesic Connectivity
parameters.description: Menu to select the connectivity between the geodesic sphere points.

parameters.name: Grid Connectivity
parameters.label: Grid Connectivity
parameters.description: Menu to select the connectivity between the grid sphere points.

parameters.name: Line Type
parameters.label: Line Type
parameters.description: Specifies whether lines should be made of line strip primitives or lines primitives.

parameters.name: Orientation
parameters.label: Orientation
parameters.description: Sets the axis for the sphere. Poles of sphere align with orientation axis.

parameters.name: Modify Bounds
parameters.label: Modify Bounds
parameters.description: Available only when an input is connected to the POP to set bounds for the POP. When Modify Bounds is On the parameters below will further modify the shape of the POP.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description: Sphere radius.

parameters.name: Frequency
parameters.label: Frequency
parameters.description: Controls the subdivision of the geodesic sphere, a higher frequency means more subdivisions.

parameters.name: Fuse
parameters.label: Fuse
parameters.description: Fuses together the different subdivided triangles that make up the geodesic sphere.

parameters.name: Fuse Technique
parameters.label: Fuse Technique
parameters.description: Technique used to fuse the subdivided triangles.

parameters.name: Anchor U
parameters.label: Anchor U
parameters.description: Puts the left side, the middle or the right side at the origin 0.

parameters.name: Anchor V
parameters.label: Anchor V
parameters.description: Puts the bottom side, the middle or the top side at the origin 0.

parameters.name: Anchor W
parameters.label: Anchor W
parameters.description: Puts the back side, the middle or the front side at the origin 0.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description: Translate the points in the three axes.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: Rotate the points around the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. Angles are given in degrees.

parameters.name: Uniform Scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: Specifies a uniform scale factor in all axes.

parameters.name: Normal
parameters.label: Normal
parameters.description: Choose whether to create a normal attribute and the attribute class of the normal attribute.

parameters.name: Texture Coordinates
parameters.label: Texture Coordinates
parameters.description: Sets the attribute class where the texture coordinates should be created.

parameters.name: Texture Method
parameters.label: Texture Method
parameters.description: Determines how the texture coordinates are applied.

parameters.name: FOV Angle
parameters.label: FOV Angle
parameters.description: Determines the field of view angle for fish eye texture mapping.

parameters.name: Shared Poles Connectivity
parameters.label: Shared Poles Connectivity
parameters.description: Sets the connectivity for spheres that use shared points at the poles.

parameters.name: U Closed
parameters.label: U Closed
parameters.description: Enable closed geometry in U direction.

parameters.name: Angle U
parameters.label: Angle U
parameters.description: The angle the sphere covers along U.

parameters.name: Tex Coords Range U
parameters.label: Tex Coords Range U
parameters.description: Texture coordinate mode in the U direction.

parameters.name: Angle V
parameters.label: Angle V
parameters.description: The angle the sphere covers along V.

parameters.name: Tex Coords Range V
parameters.label: Tex Coords Range V
parameters.description: Texture coordinate mode in the V direction.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Sphere_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
